header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Human Resources & Organizational Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in insights, and collaborating with business stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve HR operations—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Facilitate {data|CEO/HR discovery} and stakeholder interviews to diagnose absenteeism drivers; {data|analyze leave data} to quantify costs
bullets.original: [not in base template — added for Bio-Techne]

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to {data|conduct data audits} on 300+ leave cases, refuting cost assumptions via evidence-based insights
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to {coord|policy redesigns} and first aid training
Serve as {ex|first point of contact} for 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, optimizing operations and product strategy via feedback analysis
Develop guides, workflows, and SOPs for 70+ cross-functional staff, improving program knowledge and reducing {coord|onboarding time by 75%}
bullets.original: Optimize cross-functional processes for 70+ staff, creating comprehensive documentation that reduced onboarding time by 75%

bullets.text: Conduct {coord|needs assessments} with 25+ global stakeholders, resulting in 3+ new learning programs best aligned with students’ needs
Facilitate {prog|training webinars} for 600+ participants, boosting product understanding and achieving a 78% client conversion rate
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming feedback data into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Supported HRBP team by piloting an Inclusive Leadership workshop for senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
Facilitated change management through a Mergers & Acquisitions project, migrating and merging employee records across systems
bullets.original: Facilitated organizational change by coordinating a Mergers & Acquisitions project and overseeing cross-functional stakeholders

bullets.text: Streamlined {coord|onboarding processes} across 3 global locations by conducting gap analyses and clarifying job responsibilities
bullets.original: Optimized onboarding across 3 global locations by coordinating process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities

bullets.text: Assisted in {ex|employee relations cases} and {coord|HR compliance activities}, ensuring alignment with local labor laws and confidentiality
bullets.original: Delivered strategic HR solutions for business priorities across employee relations, headcount, and succession planning projects

bullets.text: Developed proficiency across various {coord|HR functions}, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and {ex|customer service}, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Drafted job descriptions and researched global compensation benchmarks, supporting {coord|data-driven HR operations} decisions
Standardized interview workflows and recruiting templates, improving {coord|process consistency} and documentation across HR functions

sections.title: Role Overview
sections.content: Proactive, detail-oriented HR Specialist joining Bio-Techne’s global People Team. Supports core HR operations, {coord|employee lifecycle processes}, and cross-functional initiatives that enhance efficiency, compliance, and {ex|employee experience}.

items.text: Deliver seamless onboarding, offboarding and core HR operations processes
Audit and update employee information to maintain alignment with company standards & compliance
Serve as first point of contact for employees — day-to-day questions, system navigation, policy inquiries
Create, enhance, and standardize forms and SOPs in alignment with HR governance
{data|Conduct data audits, assist with system updates, maintain data integrity}
Identify opportunities to streamline HR processes; propose solutions and support implementation
Collaborate with internal partners on HR programs, {coord|compliance} activities and change management
Bachelor’s in HR, Business Admin, Psychology (or equivalent) ✅
1–2 years HR experience preferred ✅ (~3 yrs total)
{tools|HR systems (e.g., Workday)} — ⚠️ Jamie has Greenhouse/Rippling/ADP, not Workday
{tools|Smartsheet, Microsoft Excel} — ⚠️ Has Excel (advanced); no Smartsheet (has Airtable/Asana)
Location: Minneapolis, MN (on-site) — Jamie is Portland, OR
H1B status: Bio-Techne unknown — not in verified cache
Role is primarily HR Ops (★☆☆) — applying as a stretch
